top_signals.title: The Mortgage Hedging ‘Beast’ Is Returning to the Treasury Market
top_signals.summary: At the height of the US bond-market selloff last month, Vishal Khanduja detected something unusual.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Cerebras CEO on Delivering AI Inference at Scale
top_signals.summary: Andrew Feldman, Co-Founder & CEO at Cerebras Systems discusses AI infrastructure and the economics of inference with Bloomberg’s Tom Giles at Bloomberg Tech 2026 in San Francisco. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Standard Chartered's three 'Ifs' that stand between bitcoin and a market low
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Public asked to help select UK wildlife to appear on new banknote series
top_signals.summary: The public are being asked to give their views on a selection of wildlife, native to the UK, that will appear on the next series of banknotes in a consultation launched today.

top_signals.title: Bluesky was launched as a Twitter rival — but it's far less popular. Now it's eyeing Reddit for inspiration
top_signals.summary: "I think the public square is not the direction we want to go in...we're very inspired by companies like Reddit," Bluesky's Rose Wang told CNBC.
